history[3]: My favorite Latin place in town! Favorites: Papusas, flan, green salsa on the table, chicken tacos, napales tacos, chorizo tacos, enchiladas, and the chimichanga is huge. Great burritos and aguas frescos too! Super fast and inexpensive!
history[4]: Bryson is the best ! Ask for him if he's working! He knew all the great recommendations and suggested some delicious drinks and was bubbly and attentive! Will def be back!

history[0]:
My gf and I came here for an end of the year celebration. With such an amazing ambiance, such a beautiful view, and  premium pricing, I had very high expectations.  On a Saturday night (busiest night of the week) we ordered lobster mac&cheese and lobster bisque soup to start. To our surprise, they said they are out of lobster. What???? So i ordered mac&cheese (no lobster) and a chilled sea food platter. My girl ordered pork dish.
The sea food combo consisted of scallops which tasted like a seafood salad at a super market, seasoned shrimp which were just ok, a few oysters were pretty good, chilled salmon which wasnt very good (also tasted like a super market seafood salad). This dish was very unimpressive for a cost of $45.  My girl's pork dish didnt taste good and was way too salty. 
Our drinks were both pretty good. Glad we had something positive. 
My conclusion here is restaurant that would be fun for happy hour for drinks and an amazing view.  The entrées were definitely not worth it and for a place this fancy it was disappointing. If this restaurant was on the first floor like any other fancy steak house or sea food place, it wouldnt survive very long.  I could of went somewhere else for the $200 I spent last night.
history[1]: Came in for a Bloody Mary.  I asked if they make good ones and the waitress said yes.  All they did was use the liquor store mixer and added Vodka. It was pretty gross.  We didnt get coasters with our glasses and it didnt seem like the people at the bar really knew what they are doing.  I checked in on Yelp and got a promo that said "with check-in free dessert". I showed this to the bartender and she had no clue what I was talking about. She said she will ask and never did. I never got my dessert.  I didnt try any food but hope the kitchen is better than the bar. Service wasnt that great and neither were the drinks.
history[2]:
Very good hookah! The bowl itself is made of green apple and fruits all around it. They put a lot of effort into making their hookah top quality and use the less harmful coal. The people that work there are very nice and gave us great service. Since it was
Cold outside, we got complimentary green tea which was delicious and flavorful.  I hope this place can stick around for a long time and ill definitely come back again.
history[3]: Came here for a quick work meeting this morning and its a nice spot. Couple things: this is not a cafè where you can sit and do work, once you are done your meal, you are getting your check. The place is tiny with maybe 30 seats and if you come here on a weekend you will wait for a long time to get seated. I ordered an omelet with spinach, mushrooms and jack cheese and it was excellent.  The service was fast and great. There is always someone walking around with coffee to make sure you are topped off the minute you need a refill.  In the spring and summer they have outside seating.  There is nothing proprietary about what they serve here and I personally like the place next door better. I would come back here again because The food is good, fairly priced and the staff is friendly.
history[4]: Its a new modern design store conveniently a mile from my house.  It has an indoor garage and street parking.  I love the fact that it has most of the high end liquor in stock and has a huge wine collection to choose from.  I would recommend this location to a friend.

history[0]: Chef Kevin Sbraga knows fried chicken. My first exposure to his succulent birds was the hot chicken at The Fat Ham. Lo and behold, his lavender honey fried chicken tops its spicy cousin. The chicken is brined in buttermilk, dredged in seasoned flour and sits for ten minutes. It's then coated with a second round of flour and dropped into the fryer. The chicken (breast, wing, thigh, drumstick) is drizzled with lavender honey and sprinkled with salt and ground pink peppercorns. It comes with a buttermilk biscuit, whipped honey-thyme butter, and red bliss potato salad. It is some of the best fried chicken I've eaten in a long time. Crunchy, crispy coating on the outside with a juicy, moist inside. The sweet honey glaze is truly outstanding and finger lickin' good. The biscuit is awesome. I would definitely order this dish again and again. I got the salty but delicious fried smelts with chili pepper aioli as an appetizer. My friend Chris W got the filet mignon and butter poached king crab surf and turf. For dessert, I had a slice of salted pecan pie and he had the ice cream sundae. Every dish at Juniper Commons was top-notch. Chef Sbraga does it again!
history[1]: The Bourbon Street Breakfast is really good and a great value. It's basically a dish of red beans and rice topped with a fried egg and split-and-toasted corn muffin. I had one today and it really hit the spot! Great comfort food. I also tried the gator sausage on a stick which was really good.
history[2]: After reading about and seeing the vegetable timbale, I wanted to try one, but unfortunately I was told it was removed from the menu recently (it may return sometime soon). Instead, I ordered the portabello mushroom, red pepper, and asparagus sandwich in a tomato wrap rather than a bun. The balsamic vinegar dressing tasted really good with this combination and while the portabello mushrooms and red peppers were juicy, delicious, and fantastic, sadly the asparagus was overcooked and stringy. One bite pulled a bundle of asparagus right out of the wrap! I tried to chew through the asparagus, but it just wasn't very good. I also got a sweet potato cupcake, which I thought was very good. I'd like to try this place again because there are so many thoughtfully-prepared options. I'll just write it off as a bad day for asparagus. Easily 4 stars, but I'll give it 3 for now because of the bad day.
history[3]: HipCityVeg is a great place for meat lovers to try something outside their comfort zone. I love meat, all kinds of meat, so I didn't think I'd be satisfied with an all veggie sandwich. Govinda's is good, but after eating their chicken cheesesteaks again and again, you'll realize that you're just eating bread (seitan) with bread (the bun or wrap) with a small helping of peppers. The focus is not on the seitan or soy meat but on all of the delicious, wonderful ingredients that are married to it. For fast (or speedy) food, this is really good. The Crispy HipCity Ranch is also delish. The Bella Bistro is a big portabella mushroom sandwich. Many vegetarians will claim that portabella mushrooms taste almost exactly like beef. It doesn't. It's good, but to call it beef would be insulting to both beef and mushrooms. The Bella Bistro is a nice, hearty sandwich filled with plenty of red onions and artichokes, a couple of slices of tomato (from possibly a small beefsteak tomato), arugala, and olive tampenade all in a big multi-grain roll. Be forewarned, your breath will stink of onions when you're done, but it's sooo good. The Fajita Wrap with its blackened soy chicken, peppers, onions, pico de gallo, and avocado is a little more forgiving. The use of intense flavors in both sandwiches masks the fact that you're not eating meat. The Buffalo Bella is another yummy. I find myself ordering the Philly Steak quite often these days because of its delicious mix of soy meat, mushrooms, and onions. The Jerk Caesar Salad is my only nonfave so far. There's too much salad compared to the protein portion. Give this place a try. The servers are friendly, which makes the wait in the occasionally long lines bearable. It's not exactly a sit-down place as there is limited seating, but on a warm, sunny day, I can think of few places around Rittenhouse Square that can provide you with a more satisfying sandwich that you can enjoy while sitting in the park people-watching. My only gripe is they took away the Kid's Steak from the kid's menu. Now I can't really introduce my little ones to the wonderful sandwiches here since they're not really sized appropriately for kids.
history[4]: The first thing you'll notice when you walk into the restaurant is how tiny the space is. There are 30 seats in the restaurant and an additional 12 at the bar, but the seating is creatively arranged to allow guests and servers to move through the restaurant with ease. The decor is an odd juxtaposition of rustic meets modern. You notice the variety of whiskey bottles on the shelves behind the bar and immediately acknowledge that you're in a Southern-style restaurant. Service moves at a slow, steady pace. The first of our dishes arrives, a bed of mustard greens tossed with benne seeds and peanuts. The dressing is fiery hot and spicy good! Spicy without being unbearable, the dressing accents the nuts and greens nicely. What a crazy flavor combination! The shrimp and grits were utter perfection. Big, juicy, grilled shrimp sitting on top of a small helping of creamy, buttery grits with diced pieces of country ham strewn for good measure. Who would think grits could be this good? The buttermilk coated fried oyster slider with its burst of flavor in the middle was just sooo good! Move over crabcake and filet sliders, this oyster slider has found a place in my heart! The blackened catfish was one of the best pieces of catfish I've ever eaten. It was firm like rockfish, but didn't have that characteristic catfish taste or odor. The Carolina gold rice is one of the best accompaniments I've ordered in a while. It pairs really well with the hot chicken, which is OH. MY. GOD. everything that the hype says it is. It is indeed fiery hot, as in spicy hot. It is indeed crispy on the outside and juicy in the middle. My friend Chris W and I were ready, willing, and able to dive right into the alleged chicken that saw fit to win the heart of local food critic, Craig Laban. In our two orders, our server stated how fortunate we were to have one dish with white meat and one dish with dark meat. Without hesitation, I went for the dark meat. I tasted a small sample of the breast meat on Chris's plate and it, too, was juicy good. Almost too good. Was there crack in the chicken? The pecan pie was a delicious ending to a fantastic meal. You can't ask for a better meal anywhere. I felt like I was at Morimoto or Vetri. For this reason, I can honestly give The Fat Ham my highest rating.

history[0]: I go here after my daughters ballet class weekly.  Its a pretty small location and I wish they had family night like they do at pyramids and plainfield locations.  Food as usual is always great, and service is pretty prompt.
history[1]: I rarely travel to this side of town (westsider) but my sister suggested it for our family bday sinner. Set inside a strip mall i couldn't tell if it was even open from the outside, but once inside it was very clean and welcoming! I ordered the cajun chicken pasta and they didn't skimp ok the cheese or spices! It was DELICIOUS! The toast was well seasoned as well. Our party of 7 loved all our meals! And the prices cant be beat! Thanks
history[2]: Visited the orchid show with my 2 year old and it was a great time! wish there were more golf carts to transport us with small children from the museum to the greenhouse. We went on a first Thursday = free admission! some of the Lilly house is being renovated until May, so we didn't get to go upstairs and the pop up shop with the Orchids was double the price of Home Depot ones (which I purchased from Home Depot when I left lol) Can't wait until the Beer Garden opens at the end of this month!
history[3]: This is beautiful little Parisian dessert shop. Very intimate. The charcuterie board was underwhelming and not enough baguette for two. Definitely not worth $25. Also ordered a brownie for $5.25. Again overpriced. You're paying to say you visited. That's all. Friend ordered the quiche and thoroughly enjoyed it. The free champagne with check in was a great addition. Great spot for an after dinner dessert/drink with a friend. Cute vintage cuterly,stemware,furnishings
history[4]: An indoor bounce house. My 1.5 yr old loved it! She was a bit frightened at first bit warmed up in 10 minutes. She jumped and slid to her hearts content! Plus her age was FREE! Great times for toddlers !

history[0]:
Obviously it's starbucks and obviously their products are the same everywhere so I won't say anything about what they have, rather, how they have it.

I've been here about a million times, it's close to school and my department frequents this location.

I give three stars because it seems that there is almost Always a line.  On occasion I have waited longer than felt necessary for a drink, and I have been there at least three times when they were out of vanilla syrup.  How the heck does starbucks run out of vanilla syrup??

Anyways,  they have a great outdoor seating section, one of the best in my opinion, and the workers are normally very nice.
history[1]:
I'm giving Envie a four star for a lot of reasons, but most of all I think it's for the sentimental factor.  I'm pretty sure this is the first cafe that my husband and I visited after moving here and I was so excited about moving it makes me remember that time every time I'm in there.

Great things about Envie
1. it's on decatur where most of my favorite things are
2. it's always fun to people watch here
3. they are open fairly late compared to a lot of places (midnight)
4. their coffee is pretty good
5. they offer alcoholic coffee drinks (also pretty good)
6. the food is good, I really like the sandwiches

Not so great but okay
1. it can be really noisy since right on decatur
2. sometimes it's really crowded
3. sometimes it's hard to get the barista's attention

This place is pretty awesome try it out for lunch or try it for late night you'll have a good time.  :)
history[2]:
I have been to this cafe more times than I can count.

The first year that I lived here I was frequenting this place on the way to school nearly every day.  My trips were for one reason, the AWESOME iced coffee.  Obviously it's a cold drip coffee, it's so delicious I loved it.

Another thing that's awesome here.  The waffles.  They will basically do whatever you want and one of the best things is apples, walnuts, and peanut butter.  SO GOOD! yummy!

It kinda sucks that they close by 2 or 3, but if you like early breakfast than go go, but if you like brunch time they will be super busy and you will have to wait.  Later in the day they tend to start running out of things so make sure and get there early!

They do take credit.  :)
history[3]:
This is such a fun place too eat.  Here are some of my favorites.

Appetizers:  
Bread sticks (yummy comes with a tangy sauce that is red and delicious)
Focaccia (also quite delicious, similar to the bread sticks but with spices added on top)
Hummus (good, not the most amazing that I've ever had, but good)
Garlic sauce thing (I can't remember the name but it is really tasty)
Soup of the day I think it's the crab chowder, if they have it, get it!  It's so good!!

Salads:
Chicken salad, I really love this salad it's more than plenty for a meal so don't worry.  It comes with apples, and nuts and the dressing is a vinaigrette that is just right!    

Pastas:
Both of the baked tortelinnis are such a treat!  I prefer the pesto but both are worth a try.   

Pizza's:
I don't have a preference and if I do order a pizza I get peppers and onions and have yet to ever have a bad experience with it here.  Very good!

GO you will enjoy it!  :)

OH and I've been to all of the locations.
history[4]:
I feel like I should pull out a hula skirt and start dancing. The hula girl pizza it's like a song in my mouth. Like I open my mouth and some hawaiian music starts playing. Open song close chew open song close chew open. You get the idea. 
Delicious awesome and clearly a new favorite. I am in love with your pizza Katie.      I will come back for you I promise I won't be gone for long. Oh and your garlic fries I'm gonna need some more of that too thanks!
I'm not a traitor theos I love you too I'm just spreading the love!

history[0]:
This Companion is close to work, so I dropped in the other day for a sammy. My first thought was - it's SMALL! It gets super crowded at lunch and is also small. I wouldn't want to eat in unless you want hoards of people around and people watching you eat and begging with their eyes for you to finish already so they can take your table. Parking is actually ok since there is a side street that is easy to park on and walk since it's on the corner. 

That being said, Companion has awesome bread and sandwiches. I love the cocktail party - brie, apples and apricot marmalade on some sort of walnut bread. Toasted. YUM!! It doesn't come with anything but you can make it a bag lunch easily. The soups and entrees come with a roll or bavarian pretzel on the side. I highly recommend the pretzel. They are so good! I buy them in Schnuck's or Straubs frequently. Warm them up and they make a great snack. Companion also has local products like Fitz's. It's pretty quick once you order to get your food. 

I was pleasantly surprised to see that the foil they wrapped my sandwich in kept it really warm when I got to work and was ready to eat it. I also got a frosted sugar cookie, which I didn't like much. The frosting was kind of gummy tasting and the consistency of the cookie wasn't great. That being said, there are a lot of cool designs on them. 

This is definitely a primo sandwich stop!
history[1]:
Typical Petsmart. I haven't been much of one to frequent Petsmart because I think almost all the food/treats they sell aren't of good quality - read up on canine or feline nutrition and you'll see why. They do now sell one brand that I approve of, Wellness, which is a new thing. I don't feed my pets this brand of food but I do buy their chewy grain free treats, which my dogs (one toothless) enjoy and can eat with no problem. Their prices on toys are pretty good too, as well as supplies.

What sets this Petsmart apart is the cat room! I am impartial perhaps. One warm summer day, I wandered by and saw a cute little kitten named Audrey. I fell in love and texted her pic to my now husband. He agreed to come see her. Then I looked at the other kitties and noticed a black older cat rubbing against the bars of his cage trying to get me to pet him. Later on- we came by and they were letting the kitties out of their cages. We saw little Audrey- what a cutie! Then I asked to see the black kitty - Fillmore. They opened his cage and he JUMPED into my arms, nestling his face into my shoulder and purring like crazy. SOLD! Fillmore became my first cat ever. That was the first and last time he liked me to hold him but he still is the coolest cat in the world!

Yay Petsmart for bringing us together!
history[2]:
I've been wanting to try this place since it opened and finally got a chance on my 5th wedding anniversary! While I take my baby son out to many restaurants, this isn't the kind of place I wanted to spend a bunch of money at and have to worry about him. So we came child-free. We made a reservation, which was a good idea. It was packed! We were seated upstairs, and as some others mention, it was LOUD. It didn't help that one party seated near us was quite obnoxious - to the tune of everyone turning around and staring. While it wasn't a romantic ambiance, it was still an enjoyable meal.

We started with the roasted cauliflower and the fries with ketchup and aioli. Both were delicious. The cauliflower came with golden raisins and gremolata. The fries were thin, salty, and had delicious dipping sauces. We cleaned our plates and washed them down with beer (him) and pineapple agua fresca (me). They have a huge list of wine and spirits here, and even a sommelier. I'm abstaining from alcohol currently but my husband enjoyed his pour. We continued our meal with the burger (him) and ratatouille (me). He enjoyed the burger with more fries, though said it was a little unimaginative. I LOVED the ratatouille. It had penne pasta, eggplant, zucchini and summer squash in a red pepper tomato sauce and lots of cheese. It came with a huge nest of arugula on time, which I removed (yuck, my least favorite green). I wanted to lick my plate. The flavors were intense. I'm not even a huge fan of eggplant unless it's very well prepared, like here. We finished with desserts, the pumpkin tart with maple ice cream and the oat financier cake with caramel apples and creme fraiche. Both were again, amazing. The oat cake was so flavorful and moist, perfectly complimented by the apples and creme. My husband almost didn't want us to order two to share because he was so full, but he couldn't stop eating them either. For two drinks, two apps, two entrees and two desserts, the bill was about $75 before tip. Not bad at all for this quality of meal. 

Service was on point all night. They have a lot of staff and seem to take somewhat of a team approach. I saw many people delivering food and helping out other than servers. We rarely had empty water glasses or dirty dishes on our table for more than a minute. 

Overall, a great experience and I will be back. They start serving brunch soon and I plan on trying it. Hopefully the crowds that we saw also enjoyed it and it stays around. Great addition to Maplewood!
history[3]:
So a vegetarian walks into a meat-centric restaurant... and a four star review ensues? Yep, that's right. I like The Block a lot! Everything I've had here has been good. I've been a handful of times. The only thing I wish they had was one more vegetarian entree on the menu for variety. 

I love the happy hour here. Great drinks and food specials. My favorites are the fries and the soft pretzels with cheese sauce. Both delicious!! 

For lunch or dinner, their salads are great. They always have a special salad and it doesn't always include meat, but if so, they can modify it. Their salad dressings are great and ingredients really fresh. Half or whole portions are available for all appetites. Their mac n cheese is a good option but it was missing something to make it a little more flavorful. I loved the cheese on top. 

They have house made desserts, which are amazing!! Their homemade ice cream is to die for. Really. Don't miss it. 

Service is always good. 

I'll be back!
history[4]:
I had the pleasure of dining at Home Wine Kitchen twice in one week! The first being brunch with Erin and Tim G, and the second being a ladies night out with Stephanie S and other Twitter friends who I begged to let me join. Ha! 

First, the brunch experience. Make reservations. We didn't because it was a spur of the moment decision, and so we sat at the high tops, which aren't so bad if you're tall, but for someone short whose legs don't touch the floor it's not that comfy. Lesson learned for next time. I had a mimosa, which was refreshing, though maybe a little tart for my taste. Then I had the crepes. OMG. They were unbelievable. The best I've ever had, hands down. Stuffed with marscapone and covered in fresh fruit (raspberries, strawberries and cherries), they were really filling. I couldn't finish them. Perfect amount of sweetness but not over the top. They had several vegetarian options for brunch. The price was good, befitting of the portion and quality of ingredients. I will definitely be back for this.

For dinner, we made reservations so no problem there. They can accommodate groups, even though they are small (take note, Farmhaus!!). We had 6 people and there was another group of 7-8 when we were there too.

The menu changes every week, so be sure to check it out if you have dietary restrictions. We made reservations in advance and with me being vegetarian and another dining partner being vegan, we had no problem. Lots of veggies and they can also do some things without meat if you ask.

I ordered the roasted veggie salad first. It was brussels sprouts and something else that I didn't know what it was, but was good, in an herbed butter sauce, served warm. YUM! I only wish it had more brussels sprouts - LOVE them! Then I ordered polenta with vegetables for my entree. Again, yummy! The polenta was the perfect consistency and tasted great with the veggies (asparagus, summer squash, zucchini) and was drizzled with this absolutely delicious red pepper saffron sauce that I wanted to lick off of my plate. My only complaint about the dish was that it was really small for $18.

We had two bottles of wine between the group. I am a wine idiot so I can't say much about them, though one thing that makes it unique is that no matter which wine you order, it's $8 a glass and $30 a bottle. Definitely a good deal for the bottle if you have enough people or want to get a buzz!

Wait staff was very efficient both time and nice too, and Josh, one of the owners, was very visible, which I like. Cassy, the chef, came out during dinner and talked to several tables. I like the small, family type atmosphere, and seeing the chef and owner involved and caring for their patrons is a huge plus in my book.

Both times, it was really hot. I mentioned this to Josh, the owner, and he said that they have had the AC people come out but it's working fine. Must just be the space. 

I'll be back for sure, though on a cooler day!

history[0]: It's cool and it's free... why not check it out?  They change the content frequently and have a great show for the end of year holidays.  I guess the only downside is it can get extremely packed and seating is limited.  Get their early unless you don't mind standing or sitting on the floor.
history[1]:
Still without a sign, this Han location might be a bit difficult to find.  We managed to spot it and doubled back.  Unfortunately, the kitchen had just closed.  Luckily, they were willing to prepare us something to go.  We kept it simple on them and ordered the dan dan noodles.  

Don't take the 3-star review the wrong way, the food was great.  However, it seemed no where near as good as the Manayunk location, which I also tried shortly after it first opened.  Considering it was to-go and knowing Han doesn't put out 3-star food, I'll be back (when the kitchen is open) to give them another shot at boosting the score.
history[2]:
Nataly N continued to amaze me for my birthday weekend with another surprise dinner.  This time, we dined alone ;)

Aldine had been on my to-try nearly since it opened.  They offer three courses, plus dessert, and drinks via their a la carte menu.  As I've always preferred a variety of food versus a huge portion of one dish when dining out, their type of menu plays to my preferences.  

We each began with a cocktail and first.  I cannot recall the name of my cocktail, but you'll know it as the one with tequila, watermelon, and a chilli pepper spice.  The spice was not overpowering, but did help ensure I didn't down the drink in one go!  As for our firsts, we shared the beets and corn + truffle custard.  The beet salad came with a cashew butter that helped balance the ingredients.  The custard appeared simple and straight forward, but after tasting I realized it had 'a lot going on' with bits of mushroom and tomato.

Instead of each ordering a second, we split the heirloom tomato.  Like the previous salad, it was well assembled and presented.  I could share more notes, but it went down so quickly!

We ended the savory dishes by sharing the culotte steak and pork coppa.  I was surprised just how well the blueberry paired with the pork and black garlic chips that came with the steak ensure each bite was excellent.  No worries about temperature as they were both cooked perfectly.

We ended the night with dessert, including a well place candle arranged by Nataly N.  Their olive oil cake was rather simple but nonetheless delicious.  The panna cotta was smooth and very easy to enjoy.

To round out my review, I can touch on service and atmosphere.  It was easy to say service was amazing.  Prompt coverage without interrupting our alone time.  Greeting smiles with great recommendations. On atmosphere, yes the dining area was very basic.  However, it was also very bright and filled with natural light as we dined just before sundown.  I feel it helped us be ourselves and feel comfortable while enjoying our fine dining we didn't need to lift a finger to prepare...
history[3]:
Heads up, Honey's is Cash Only!

Having heard all the craze for Honey's, I finally managed to make it out for a brunch. The place was packed yet seating us didn't take more than 20 minutes. They had tons of specials, most of which caught my eye, so I honestly didn't take a deep dive into the menu.

I went with lunch and the special bbq chicken quesadilla. It had a ton of veggies, lots of tasty cheese and chicken, and was lightly covered in an avocado sour cream.  While it was sizable, it didn't quite fill me up.  My table also had the key lime french toast, which looked awesome and tasted good as well.  Be warned, it was a bit tart.  We all enjoyed every bite and will be back for another brunch sooner rather than later!
history[4]:
I certainly felt out of place not being from 'Nova and it wasn't even game day!  Still, once I got over I ain't preppy enough to be here feeling, I ended up having a really good time.

Like every bar in America, they have different drink specials on different nights of the week.  Since I had never been to Kelly's, I had no idea what was on special.  After ordering something that wasn't on special, the bartender was kind enough to let me know what I could get cheaper that taste nearly the same, despite the line forming behind me.  

Cheap drinks, three floors to dance, a DJ, and a good crowd.  I'm sold.

history[0]: Well if you like customer service then this is your kind of place. As soon as you walknin the door you get that lovely sound "can I help you find something"! You never have to hunt somebody down. They always have most everything the big box retailer stores have or they will get it for you. Oh and the newest thing is they can replace your high-tech chip car keys for a fraction of the cost.
history[1]: Tanya and Matt took over from I think it was a TCBY and have really created a local following for this nice little shop. I have to admit that Ice Cream is my down fall and they have enough flavors to keep an ice cream snob like myself happy, and if you are looking for something a little healthier then you can go low cal yogurt. They have just about every type of topping that you can think of. They have had live music on the weekends and they even have a little play area for the kids, plus some nice chairs to sit outside if the weather permits. Oh and if you are hiding your fetish for ice cream they have a drive through so you can wear dark glasses and no one will know its you (not that I have do that). Overall its a great experience.
history[2]: Wondering about this online check in - the app said zero wait and I show up and it's a 40 minute wait? Something not right between the app and the salon! Overall its a nice place. I have had my hair cut by two of the stylist and for an $18 dollar Haircut and shampoo not a bad job. I would definitely recommend them but watch coming an hour before and after lunch as its really busy unless you have checked in online.
history[3]: This is a place that really needs no review. It is hands down the best chicken sandwich. And their shakes are pretty good to. We have used this one to cater a few parties including my three year old's birthday party. They are friendly and helpful and I will continue to be a repeat customer.
history[4]: Two Word - BIG Fan- We are wine drinkers in my house and this is as good as it gets. An amazing selection and amazing staff to help you find what you are looking for or even what your not looking for. The prices are very reasonable and they continually get new things in so I would tell you go unto the garden of grapes and find your self a bottle of Grüner Veltliner (Aka) the Austrian version of Sauvignon Blanc - (Long Story) and enjoy as they have a great selection. Oh and another alternative white they carry from Portland Oregon called Evolution - Amazing. Oh and I am normally a red wine drinker. Cheers!

history[0]: Looking for the  best Italian food and grocery? This is the place to be.  It could be very crowded on weekend . The full parking lot and hundreds of customers   inside the store proved that how popular this place is. Regardless of the big crowd the service was fast. The employee were well trained and friendly.  They were on the top of their game. I love the prepared food area. This time we got 2 Mango Tandoori chicken drumsticks, a piece of italian stromboli  and couple piece of beef plus 2 root beer sum up around $10. We asked the food to be heated and it took less than 5 minutes. We had a delicious picnic in the park afterward.  This is the best Italian market in my book. I never got disappointed every time I came here.
history[1]:
To maximize the hot pot experience: come with a group of friends, take your time and prepare your favorite sauce.
Our group of 7 people occupied the big round table so we got enough table space for all the materials . I tried Coty S sauce recipe. It turned out quite delicious , I loved it. We ordered, cooked or grilled, ate, enjoyed the conversation. After 2 hours, my tummy was happy. We were each served a complementary small Yogurt drink. The services were good. They added venting vacuum on top of each small table, so the grilling was less smoky . Overall the materials were fresh . The selections were wide.  I enjoyed the hot pot meal.
history[2]: We drove by this place and decided to check it out . Although it's already  past their closing time 7 p.m. , some customers were still in there. So they sold  us a cup of Halo Halo.  It had all the goodies -jello, flan, ice cream, shred ice, sweet beans,  sweet  condensed milk etc. It was a   rich, delicious dessert, big enough for two people to share. Next time I will come not too late and try other dessert treat.
history[3]: Great ambient here! I specially liked their surround  sound system. The music came out from different corners of the room. The crystal clear, high fidelity sound quality make me feel like the musicians were playing right there. The black and write color theme of the interior looked modern. The food and services were great ,too. We had salad (came with the entrée, I liked the fresh fruit on top), blacken salmon with mango, island delight (came with plantain, johnny cake - a fried dough, bean and rice, curry goat, ox tail and jerk chicken). The flavor was rich and the meat was tender. Beautifully decorated  rum cake and coconut cake were moist and fresh , tasted wonderful.
history[4]: This restaurant located at Tampa Palm in a plaza  behind Olive Garden . The interior wasn't so fancy or big but it was clean and comfortable. It sits about 20 people.   I had the beef drunken noodles lunch special and shrimp roll appetizer (for 7.50). Both tasted very  good. Service was fast . If you like spicy, ethnic Thai food, this place is worth trying.

history[0]: Such a breath of fresh air and a relief from the numbingly bad diners that we have here in Largo Clearwater. This place is excellent beautiful clean and the food is so well presented and so very good. Fresh ingredients friendly waitstaff outdoor dining and mimosas you can't beat it.
history[1]: Came on a Sunday had some bloody Mary's and the soup salad special. It was a lot of fun and I will be back service was good food was good atmosphere was good a really nice time was had by all.
history[2]: Fantastic sushi and great happy hour. $2 beers! All the rolls I've had are fresh and tasty. My only gripe is they charge extra for ponzu sauce and extra ginger. That's a turnoff.
history[3]: For under 5 bucks, coffee-2 eggs- homefries and toast. Can't beat it in my book. I'm surprised and pleased at the high quality of the food.
history[4]: This is mine and my husbands first time here. The food was really spectacular we had omelets with three ingredients toast and a side. The only weird thing about it is that they only give you one slice of toast. Hence the four stars. If they were to give me that extra slice of toast it would be a five-star experience.

history[0]:
The little add-on to Delicia.  I would imagine most people come here for a drink while waiting on their table for Delicia.  They have very similar food, and nearly identical drinks (besides the signature ones like Fire N Ice) 

We ate here once before and the food was good.  Bartender is not overwhelmed and therefore very friendly and talkative.  Drinks are made well and cost about %25 less than next door at the restaurant. :)

Would I come here purely for La Mulita? nope....but I'd come here while waiting for my table at Delicia for sure. :)
history[1]:
So I have been a member here for a few months now...I originally started out with a Groupon for 2 weeks and eventually signed up.  This is an insane workout.  You do 15 minutes of "warm up" which I put in quotes only because for most people this "Warm Up" will put them on the floor sucking wind.  After that, you are given 1 minute to put your gloves on and begin a 3 minutes, 8 round session where you follow the instructors command on hits.  This portion, you honestly get what you put into it.  I've seen people just tap the bag and some stand there and not really go after it....I understand the "warm up" was tough, but come on...hit the thing! :)

After the 8 rounds, you grab a medicine ball and begin Core Strength workout for the remaining 10 minutes of the class.

All in all, you are there for 60 minutes and tend to burn 600-1000 calories (depending on output and fitness level)

It is a great way to get in shape and I do love the workout.  1 Star off for the price.  It is roughly $70/mo if you sign up for a year but then they want to try and get you to pay 25$ for 30 minute private training sessions.  I don't like that...but hey, they have a business to run I guess.

I highly recommend you try a free class...maybe I'll see you there!
history[2]:
This place is a fun spot off the main Broadripple strip.  It tends to be a mid-30s crowd which is a needed break from the 21 crowd of Broadripple.  This place feels like it belongs on Mass Ave, or Fountain Square.

I come here for the Bread Pudding dessert.  Delicious! :) Although this time they put way too much honey on it which stole the flavor.  Good beer selection as well.
history[3]:
Big fan!  I honestly only came in because they sent a $10 off coupon in the mail and I was hungry one day during lunch.  When you go in, it has a diner feel for sure.  The menu has pretty much everything you'd expect in the breakfast department and the lunch menu has classic diner fare as well as other american favorites.  I was not there during dinner but they usually have a special of the day listed up on the board for dinner according to the general manager who came over to speak with me.

I ordered the philly cheese steak but substituted the fries for hash browns with onions and cheese as I am a sucker for Waffle House hash browns and wanted to compare these.

First off...be ready for absurd portion sizes.  The "sandwich" that came out was basically 2 sandwiches. Instead of a place taking a sandwich and cutting it in half, it feels like this place just put 2 sandwiches and placed them next to each other to resemble it cut in half.  It was good.  Not amazing in philly cheese steaks standards, but I was not expecting perfection from a diner.  I think it was still a solid sandwich.

The hash browns, nearly filled the whole plate (which was a massive plate) as well.  They could have been lunch on their own.  While they do not take the crown away from Waffle House in my opinion, they were close.  That combined with the fact this location is close to me, makes me happy that I can come here for good hash browns.

I'll definitely be back here again when I'm craving breakfast foods...or if I'm with someone else and we want to share 1 item. :)
history[4]:
This place is a lot of fun on a mild day sitting outside.  Upland has recently shot up my beer charts with their Sours.  They have 4-5 now which always keeps me interested in coming back.  Besides the delicious beers, their food is also very good.  I really enjoy the fish and chips here.

Come here with some friends, sit on the patio outside and enjoy your beer of choice...cannot go wrong with this place!

history[0]:
Excellent selection!

Gilley's has both an antique section (and barn!) and a newer craft section. I can only speak for the antique section. There are loads of booths with something for everyone. Be sure to check it out if you like antiquing.
history[1]: Pretty good.  My husband is more of a fan than I am.  The service here can be a bit lacking, but the product they produce is consistent and good.  We especially like their cute heart-shaped Valentines' pizzas.
history[2]: I liked this place.  The young man behind the counter was friendly and efficient.  The product was creamy and delicious.  I had the apple pie a la cold stone.  It had plenty of apple chunks as well as crust pieces in it.  The only reason I am deducting one star is for the price.  Dang that's some expensive ice cream!
history[3]: Good home cookin' cafeteria-style.  pies and desserts, fried chicken, grilled chicken, you name it. My recent favorite would have to be the grilled chicken with potatoes, onions, and peppers. Amazing, and also a healthier option.
history[4]:
Fast, friendly, and efficient.  They always greet you at the door, which is nice.  The food and the ice cream treats are made to order.  I had a burger on  sourdough bread.  I thought the bread had a slightly odd taste to it, even for sourdough.  But my burger was good.  For dessert, I got a concrete mixer made with the flavor of the day (S'mores), Oreos, and caramel.  It was really good.  Packed with good stuff. 

I would come back here again.  I just wouldn't order the sourdough melt.

history[0]:
We see Dr. Gloudemans, and she's a wonderful doctor. Friendly, upbeat, up on the most recent research, and pro-vaxx (hooray!!). She's proactive about problems after making sure things are worth worrying about-- "let's keep an eye on this, and if there's still a problem in a month or two, come back in and we'll see about a referral to a specialist."

As others have mentioned, we've had a few billing hiccups. We received a bill saying we were past due on 3 appointments' fees, despite paying all copays in the office. Luckily it was all straightened out after a phone call, but it's been frustrating on that front.
history[1]: Best thrift store in Tucson! Lots of selection, and there's usually a particular color tag that's 50% off each day. The store is roomy and well-lit, though the last time I went it seemed a little dingy and chaotic. Overall, a great place to look for clothes you'll only use once (Halloween costumes, coats for trips out of town, etc.), rarely-used small appliances, and the like.
history[2]: Best wings in Tucson by far!! My husband and I go there maybe once a week, he goes for spicy and I get "honey hot" (spicy BBQ). The wings are nice and meaty with just the right amount of sauce, and when you buy by the dozen, the prices are very reasonable.
history[3]:
I haven't been a hardcore band geek for well over 10 years, but stepping into the store brought it all back. Instruments, beginner books from every curriculum possible, reeds, cleaning kits, they've got it all! 
The sheet music selection for piano was fairly limited and weighted towards the "easy arrangement" pop collections, but special orders go out on Mondays and they can get you pretty much anything you're looking for. They can also print licensed sheet music for single songs for a few dollars, and that system has a HUGE selection.
The store has pretty much anything a band geek or orch dork could look for, and the staff is super-helpful to boot. Highly recommended!!
history[4]:
We've boarded our 2 dogs here (1 big, 1 small) many times for weekend trips, and the dogs LOVE staying at the Pets Hotel. The staff gives the dogs lots of attention, so nobody ever feels neglected, and our dogs love the group playtime.
The staff does offer lots of addons, like extra playtime and treats, which can get expensive, but our dogs do just fine with the standard socialization time, and they'll give your critters regular old Milk Bones each day if you ask nicely.
Overall, we've had nothing but good experiences boarding our dogs here, 
and we'll definitely keep bringing them back!

history[0]:
BLUF:  Great food, great owner and staff.  Setup can be annoying.

Small place but with lots of seating but not enough sometimes for how popular it can be.  You walk in and go to the register where you look at the menu, order and pay.  Then you look for a seat.  This can be annoying and not group friendly.  Sometimes you will wait and wait and wait and almost get your food with no where to sit.  It hasnt happened to me but I am a spaz and always think it will.

The food is great.  Last time I got the jalepeno pork over grits.  It was fantastic and not too spicy.  Very very good.  My lady got the smoked salmon omelet with fried capers and it was really good too.  Very great food and interesting menu with the standard breakfast options as well.

The owner is moving around the floor the whole time and he is really really nice.  All of the staff is nice.  

My only real comlaint is that for me the ordering/waiting thing is a little stressful and can sometimes stop you from even enjoying your food lesiurely cuz a million people are standing there looking for the next available table.

Great food though.
history[1]:
Greatest place on earth?  Yes.  

I love this place.  I love diner food. Every time I go I get the country fried steak breakfast and I am never disappointed.  Very cheap for a ton of food.  And the staff is always super nice.  I really love this place.  It is open all night so it is never a bad time to go.  Everything on their menu is always available.  I love this place so much.  I just got back so now I am going to take a nap...
history[2]: It is pizza and they serve beer.  Nothing too special but can hit the spot.  It is chain which knocks it down in my opinion.  That being said I still eat it a couple times a year because will hit the spot.
history[3]:
BLUF:  Great food and service.  Fancy atmosphere and gorgeous dinning area.  Great happy hour.  Make a reservation.  Get a sazerac.  

Went there on a sunday early dinner.  Made reservations and glad I did cuz it filled up shortly past 6.  Got there just in time for the happy hour deals.  Half off oysters which were huge and amazing.  Half off cocktails; the sazerac was one of the better ones I have had in the city (not too sweet).  

Waiter was great.  No complaints.  Atmosphere was intimate yet still a full house.  The actual restaurant was huge with super high ceilings.  Very nice place.

I got the shrimp and grits.  They were jalapeno cheese grits and jumbo shrimp with a brown gravy.  It was amazing.  Not a huge portion though so I suggest getting appetizers or dessert too.

My lady got the squash crepes (maybe?) and she really liked it.  Bro-in-law got fish cuz he couldnt eat anymore fried foods that weekend (sissy).  He liked his too. 

All the portions were a little small.  i know that is a very "american" and crass observation/opinion cuz size doesn't matter...but too bad; I like to be in pain after dinner especially when spending $30 an entree.  A true foodie would not note this but i am not a professional, I just want to eat.

Overall the place is great.  Def recommend going to knock if off your list and get some great food.  I don't think I would become a regular.
history[4]: Ate too much. Feel terrible. Tasted great. Thick pizza. Lots of toppings. Interesting specialty pizzas. I'll go back

history[0]: Great drinks, food and atmosphere! A little loud if you are trying to eat out with a group of friends, but still a great time! Love the tropical touch!
history[1]: Great selection. Love the clearance prices that are always around. Definitely enjoy the Starbucks inside. A little peeved at the Target Card hawkers....employees who peruse the store asking shoppers if they "want to save 10%?" I think I have been asked 50 times(by the same employee), I already have 2 Target Cards thank you very much and I should not have to hide from employees.
history[2]:
We had a great time at the museum. Hadn't been since they renovated last and was duely impressed. We actually ran out of time before we got to see everything. Will have to come back another day just to explore the grounds and see the floors we missed. 

I was a little disappointed at the security. Our phone accidently went off (we forgot to turn it off in the excitement of being there) and we were quick to turn it off after the accident, but a snobby guy came up to us and was sure to let us know that we were in the wrong (which we are aware of -thank you) we were taking care of the situation, no need to further embarass us. And to top it off he had an awful phony British accent and kept floating around the area with his nose in the air. Some people just take the their job too seriously.

Other than that we had a great time and will be sure to return, especially to try the new cafe and restaurant!
history[3]: Our office tried this pizza place for an office luncheon after receiving some coupons in the mail. We were actually pretty impressed! Breadsticks are great, as is the accompanying nacho cheese. Pizzas are good as well. What really caught my eye was the branding. You do not normally see great branding on small pizza chains, but someone really did a great job here. The slogans on the boxes and napkins were quite humorous and made me even want to keep one to take home and show my hubby. LOL, pretty impressive for a pizza brand. Not a huge fan of the tootsie rolls in the pizza box next the pizza though....they get all gummy next to the hot pizzas...yuck! Wish they would just include some in the bag with the napkins and the plates!
history[4]: I am always a little leery of department store optical places, but I was pleasantly surprised when I went here. Ever since my normal optical place stopped carrying my insurance I have had the hardest time finding a place...but Target optical is open later in the day, on the weekends and is conveniently located in one of my favorite places to shop so I thought -why not? The doctor was quick, thorough and very good at explaining everything to me without making me feel stupid.

history[0]: What's not to like? Craft brews and wine, plenty of seating, giant twinkle lights and music! I love this indoor/outdoor space. It's a great extension to the restaurant- a secret little hideaway in the middle of downtown. Mini pizzas on the menu and over 40 beers on the menu including a solid selection of local beers and locally produced wines.  Definitely a fan!
history[1]: Those that know me well are quite aware of my low aptitude for beer. Sure I know my wines, but now, I'm thinking it's time to really understand beer. With the beer boom going on in the old pueblo, I don't tire of seeing more breweries hop on the scene, and Ten Fifty-Five Brewing is doing many things well! This small operation started out in a home kitchen and has wowed locals since it's debut in Tucson. They have four beers in constant rotation and plan to have six by November. And so far, so good! Actually, great! What I find especially great is the variety. They really make beer for many palates to enjoy. I typically steer away from IPAs, so lucky for me, the Lazy Duck (American Wheat) and Leap Pale Ale are light and crisp. The Leap Pale Ale is their flagship brew and in my humble opinion, a perfect beer for those that don't want a super hoppy beer. It's just the right amount! I tasted two other beers in my flight, which are not necessarily going to always be available in their rotation. The XOXO Coffee Stout (made with coffee beans from EXO Roasters) and the Pukka Bitter (British style Ale). I enjoyed both. They also make a double IPA, the Two Sons Citra, which was sold out. As mentioned earlier, this operation is quite small and carefully run by two owners, John Vyborny and Chris Squires. Both are super passionate about their craft and they're pouring some mad love into their nano-brews. Tasting hours are limited, so make sure to visit them Thursday and Friday 4pm-7pm and Saturday 2pm-7pm. They've recently expanded their space and now have more seating, a huge chalkboard menu and a tasting bar. Stop by and have flight! Meet the owners and have a chat. While small, it's cozy and welcoming and all beer drinkers-beginning or expert will be in a hoppy (pun intended) place upon first sip. Prices start at $2 for 6oz pours, growlers are $8. If you can't make it into the brewery for a visit, you can find their beer at several local restaurants including Playground, The Good Oak, La Cocina, Tap & Bottle, Zona 78 Hacienda del Sol, Pasco and Wilko. Cheers!
history[2]:
Fresh certainly understands the meaning of the the term Fresh and has successfully embraced and implemented the use of fresh ingredients into all of their menu items. Carefully selecting their fruit for their smoothies, ensuring it is at its peak ripeness and freshness, and working closely with local vendors to bring the best quality into their kitchen, I have no doubt that Fresh will be a top favorite among locals. They may not have the largest menu with complex offerings, but that is exactly what I like about this place. I've had the pleasure of enjoying the Strawberry Chicken Salad, the Chicken Tacos, The Panino with Chicken, roasted Peppers and aioli, and a blissful sip of the smoothie my boyfriend ordered. (p.s. all this food was consumed over 2 visits). Th, the only word that came to mind with every bite was "fresh." It is rare to be wow'd by the intense freshness of ingredients when eating out. The strawberries on the salad were bright red and juicy as can be, the tacos were packed with chipotle flavor yet light, the panino was decadent yet simple, and the smoothie was all tropical, tangy freshness! 

Simple menu + fresh handpicked ingredients + passion = FRESH= perfection
history[3]: First things first, this is a dining establishment within the Ritz Carlton, so like all resorts a meal comes with a fairly high price tag. I know this going in...so...aside from that what's not to enjoy about spending the day relaxing poolside with a gorgeous view to the Arizona desert and the loveliest most polite staff in the Tucson city limits? Turquesa is the poolside restaurant at the resort. The menu covers your standard Latin fare from tacos to Nachos, salads, guacamole, ceviche and more. After two days at the pool we determined our favorite item were the Nachos. A GIANT bowl that's easily big enough for 6 people is about $11 with all the fixings- cheese, jalapeños, nachos, a TON of cheese, and your choice of chicken or meat. These nachos are legit. Sure, the green chile tacos were tasty and the grape fruit and avocado salad was certainly crispy and refreshing, but a giant bowl of nachos with a buckets of Tecate light can't be beat. Ultimately, the staff at Turquesa makes it worth while. The team here is all smiles and seriously at your service. Mac and Joel are two in particular that were so wonderful to our group. They remembered us by name the next day too! And our orders. I love staying at the Ritz for a staycation and the team at Turquesa makes the poolside experience totally fabulous.
history[4]:
We chose the Ritz Carlton for our most recent staycation over Memorial Day weekend. Due to the high volume of guests at the resort, they opened Cayton's up for dinner on a Sunday night. We had a pretty large group, so we played it safe and went with the restaurant that could accommodate us all, and they certainly did! Upon arrival we were directed to a private dining room. From a service stand point, I'd say it was a five-star experience. The staff was super friendly and extremely patient with our rowdy group. The food on the other hand was A-Ok. Cayton's menu lists over ten different gourmet burgers, salads, sandwiches and even adult shakes, including one called THE DUDE. That alone might we be worth an additional star. 

Menu items come with catchy names like "Home on the range" (a buffalo burger), the Belushi and the No Substitutions ( a quarter pound burger with three cheeses, pancetta, bacon and a bunch more). Burgers are no more expensive than Zin Burger. I ordered the Home on the Range buffalo burger which was served with oaxaca cheese, guacamole, and cilantro mayo with a side of tater tots. I should've gone with the Belushi. The patty was bright red and definitely appeared undercooked for my taste, but considering it was a buffalo patty, which tends to be leaner, I'm guessing that was the intention of the kitchen. Several others in our group ordered the Jabroni which was loaded with two patties, pancetta, capicola, mozzarella and a few other items I can't recall. It was definitely a heavy choice!  The hit of the night was definitely the DUDE shake. 

Since we were there at night we didn't get to catch the views of the golf course. The restaurant itself is certainly upscale. Since I don't golf, I don't imagine myself returning here for a meal anytime soon. It appeared as though the bathrooms also served as locker rooms for women, which were not very clean. Perhaps this was an oversight, given that they opened up on an off-night. 

Over all, Cayton's was a fun choice for our group. We were especially thankful for the shuttle to and and from the resort, otherwise we might have ended up on the 10th green...alone...in the dark.

history[0]: Big fan of this place and don't know why it's not overflowing with people. Aside from the many delicious burger/sandwich items on the menu, you can create your own burger with a huge array of options to choose from, including: bison, wild boar and elk! The space inside is open, light and airy, a little kitschy albeit. They have a bar in the back and a few TVs also spaced around the restaurant. Servers were friendly and attentive. Great place for a good quality burger.
history[1]: Had a really pleasant experience my first time here. It's a really quick walk from the Girard ave. subway stop. The space is small and eclectic. There are just 2 hair stations and 1 sink. The front of the space is a vintage/resale section with an assortment of clothing and decorative items for sale. I was offered a drink (water, coffee, tea, beer, wine) while waiting and then met with Bridget. I told her what I didn't like about my last haircut at a different salon and she was attentive. She gave me a great cut and blow-dry and went back in after the blow-dry to touch up the angles. I paid $45 for a wash, cut and blow-dry. Will definitely be back!
history[2]: Came here for a late brunch on a holiday so it was a plus that they were open. Small space, maybe 10 tables, but it's got a homey feel. I didn't realize it was Australian before reading the menu so we ordered the now infamous (thanks to Starbucks) flat whites to start. Got the dill scrambled eggs with a side of turkey bacon and we ordered the french toast with brie and apricot glaze for the table. Everything was delicious and portions were generous. I thought the apricot glaze might be too sweet with the french toast but it was really good. This was a great neighborhood find. Heads up - it's cash only.
history[3]: I had a really great experience at Sonrisa. I came here because I was unhappy with my prior dentist and the cleaning I had at that office. I was able to get a consultation for a cavity filling with Dr. Velez in the morning to accommodate my work schedule. He offered to do the filling at that time but I had to get back to work, so they told me I could come back in at 8 am the next morning - so quick and easy. Dr. Velez was very gentle, patient and kind throughout the whole process and kept asking how I was doing to make sure I wasn't in any pain. His assistant was also friendly and kind. I would highly recommend him and Sonrisa!
history[4]: Good to catch a game, or as I did, attend a fantasy draft. The food is not anything particularly special, typical sports bar menu of sandwiches, salads, burgers, fries, etc. Some items are loaded with salt or are too dried out. The space is large and open which is nice as opposed to some other dark and dingy sports bar I have been in. The service is nothing above and beyond what I would expect of a sports bar. They're quick but not there to really enhance your experience. It can get loud in here during games.

history[1]: Went here after lunch at kitchen 519. Got a ricotta cookie and a sticky bun that we were told had a Danish filling but it was just a plain old sticky bun. Still good and def was eaten. They had a multitude of cupcakes w all diff and eclectic flavors. Id like to try one next time.
history[2]: Great hiking trail. Lots of steep hills and awesome views. Had a nice work out while enjoying the sights.

history[4]: I can't believe I have passed this spot many times and never went in. Thankfully we finally decided to get some food here and I'll def be going back for more. history[0]:
What a fantabulous date night with my favorite hometown chica at Il Giardino :-)  I was stoked about trying out this Italian restaurant for a while now because I had not yet been completely satisfied with my meal at any of the other Italian restaurants (chain or not) I've been to...and Il Giardino seemed like the hidden gem of Springhouse as it is tucked away in one of many shopping centers on that side of Bethlehem Pike.

Like Bacco, Il Giardino specializes in pizza (actually, I have yet to try Bacco's pizza, so stay tuned!), so it was time for the ultimate test:  how does its white pizza play out?  Was I setting myself up for utter disappointment, or was I going to be enlightened?  White pizza is not exactly the most complicated-looking pizza around, but I only know of one other place that makes a few versions of it spectacularly (Top Tomato in Philly!!!).  

As serendipity would have it,  I received two pizzas on this date night: one which I had ordered (prochuitto gourmet pizza) and the other that was free (Il Giardino white gourmet pizza) because the free one was apparently sent to my table by accident -- yay!!!  It was a wonderful surprise, because the pizza was fantastic.  The white pizza that eventually came out for me was also very delicious and is definitely in my top 3.  

The atmosphere, I must add, feels intimate not just because it was small (I would say that it's 1/10 the size of The Olive Garden) but because on this Thursday date night, there were people who were enjoying themselves around their respective dinner tables like families, even if they really weren't in the same family.
history[1]:
Mmm, hibachi scallops :-)

I am a sucker for scallops -- so simple, but the flavors are not.  Well, my seafood dinner started out perfectly with the Benihana punch.  Haha, I am a Malibu Bay Breeze babe, so bring on the yummy rum and fruitiness!

Well, the hibachi experience at Benihana was predictable enough, but a good predictable as hibachi consists of clean, fun entertainment.  And, of course, there was the anticipation of a yummy scallop dinner, which I indeed had.  

Not bad for the suburbs, woohoo!  

P.S.: Thank you, Benihana, for playing "Life Less Ordinary" by Carbon Leaf ANNNND "Hopeless" by KT Tunstall IN A ROW while I was waiting for my hometown girlfriends to arrive ;-)
history[2]: All I can say is that the place lives up to its name :-)  Will definitely come back once I find a time to get to Rittenhouse Square :-)
