history[0]: Not a great experience!!! I've been here a couple of times and haven't had much to complain about, I've really enjoyed their food and services, but that changed when I planned a larger group event. With the increase in guests their care of us got exponentially worse. Every other time I've been here there has only been two ladies and they seemed to handle us well no hick ups. This time I made a 15 person table the first thing that happened was when we arrived they miscounted our table setting and it took about 10 minutes to get it handled and with three different servers that were not communicating to each other we kept getting more chairs and more plates and settings that had to be removed or brought back. The service this time seemed subpar and almost crass. I don't know if it was because they knew we had to pay a 18% gratuity anyway, but they were not very hospitable and I even felt ignored, they barely looked at us and often overlooked us for other smaller groups. One serve even said "I barely saw you out of the corner of my eye" because they never looked at our table directly. The food was sloppily presented Family style which I did not expect considering it was always plated individually before, I don't mind the sharing but the way it was arranged made my event feel slapdash and childish. We were constantly having to reorder things from different servers because the other would forget. What really pushed the Brunch to a bad place was that a plate of half burnt scones was dropped off, not only did it look bad, but it smelled worse and to have that placed in front of us was unappetizing and a huge oversight by the staff. One of my guests was also horribly talked down to by the staff in a very berating way when she asked for butter for her scone the server which I think was the owner stopped and said "you are in a tea house and you don't want butter, here we use Devonshire cream and not butter for our scones" this I feel was not the way to talk to a customer and embarrass them in front of a large group.  I had high hopes for this Brunch, but my hope was sorely misplaced. I don't think I will be hosting a get together here again because the service and attention to detail was so poor.
history[1]: Holy boba! Some great stuff in a cup!!! Gotta get that halo halo all the toppings were yummy and their flan is so tasty!!! Try the a chocolate-taro smoothie aka the "Johnny" or at least they should call it that cause I order it too much.
history[2]: Great handmade coffee, the owners are so nice and they make you feel like your on a coffee break at their house, the chocolate orange coffee tastes like your sipping your way through a orange grove. I took a iced coffee and they made it perfect so that even after hours the ice wouldn't affect the taste. Watch out for the giant dummy near the bathrooms though haha.
history[3]: Horrible service center staff, it took 4 hours for an oil change even though I was the first appointment of the day. If you don't drive a Cadillac they kick you aside, even if your car was purchased from them. I sat in their waiting room for hours as I saw car after car left even though they had parts replaced tires rotated balanced and all manner of things that I'm sure take longer than a simple oil change. This wasn't a one time occurrence either this long wait time madness has happened multiple times and I gave up on them and their horrible service staff and will never be back. They are not courteous or helpful they are rude and ridiculous. Even after a complaint their service manager lied to my face telling me typically it takes 4 hours to change oil and continued to just make up nonsense excuses for the reason behind the long wait but completely ignored the comments I had about other Cadillac owners who left shortly after they arrived for more major repairs than an oil change.
history[4]: Having grown up nearby to Alex's this is the BBQ I judge most BBQ against. Everything is great here. I love having the big boy platter or some Brunswick stew, but my go to food is a full rack of ribs with cole slaw and fried okra! Southern heaven in the plate. You know it's going to be great just after you walk in and are hit with the beautiful aroma of smoke and pork. Try anything and you won't be disappointed, just make sure you slather it in their delicious sauce!!!

history[0]:
No better department store in the world.  A Texas tradition.....they publish the world famous NM Christmas Catalog....Never seen it? You should.

Great service in the Tampa store at International mall.
history[1]:
Food and service were excellent, 

We had a Country Omelette, and the Wildflower Signature Scrambled plate. 

The omelet was large and truly a masterpiece, with really fresh mixed greens on the side.

The Signature Scramble was basic, but good, a little on the small side. For most people it would be just enough. 

There is plenty of parking, efficient service, friendly hostess and big picture windows. 

The prices! Inexpensive! Really a nice surprise.

Tip: They have home fried potatoes, but only on Sundays. 

See the photo of the dining room I posted.
history[2]:
Doesn't get any better than this!  

Guys need to keep their feet in good shape to wear flip flops. 

Nothing worse than bad looking toenails, and cracked feet when you have slaps on your feet!  

This is THE place for a regular guy to take care of his feet. Do it for yourself, your girl, and the general public that has to look at those ugly old dogs when you wear flip flops.
history[3]:
We had:
Fried Chicken livers with pepper jelly,
Side order of cornbread
Soup made with rice and green
Mac and Cheese
Gumbo File soup
Shrimp etouffe......

Everything was good to great.  We especially liked the chicken livers with pepper jelly. It is inexpensive and has a traditional style New Orleans dining room.
history[4]:
It pains me to write a review update, for a restaurant I have been to before (where I had a memorable experience) and reduce the rating. 

I get to New Orleans 5 to 6 times per year, and this time I was craving the fried chicken livers with sweet hot pepper jelly....previously it was amazing...

The livers were crisply fried, looked beautiful,  and were properly cooked....HOWEVER  the sweet hot pepper jelly turned out to be watery, runny, zero flavor without heat....

Sidenote: Service was excellent.

I asked my waiter to bring another small dish of pepper jelly....he checked and told me that it was all the same. ....
Disappointing. 

With so many places to eat here, I  have to think hard about coming back

history[0]:
This a really price store with great clothes. Mostly high end stuff, everything here is designer. 

The workers here are really friendly and not pushy. They will leave you alone if you just want to be left alone, they are never breathing down your neck trying to make a sale. They carry casual clothing but I think it's much better place for suits and dress shirts and fancy clothing. I think this place stands out in West Edmonton Mall in terms of service and dressy clothing for men. They really do everything to make your shopping experience better. 

Be prepared to spend some coin here, although you won't be disappointed.
history[1]:
Mary Brown's tries to be KFC but lets face it.. no chicken place can compare toe greatness of the chicken, gravy, fries or burgers at KFC.

I found their chicken to be dry and a bit tasteless. The gravy wasn't great either. In all honesty, this place didn't really have a chance because from the moment I stepped in I was comparing it to KFC in every aspect and I love KFC when it comes to things chicken related. 

Overall everything here is just sub par. Nothing to write home about and nothing to even recommend.
history[2]:
La Copa is a good store that carries soccer jerseys. Whether you are looking for international or Euro jersey's this is a pretty good spot.

They also carry training clothes and soccer gear but it's best for jerseys. I found they didn't have to big of a selection and the most popular jerseys are always sold out here or not in stock. 

You'd probably have better luck at a place like Free Kick or even ordering online.
history[3]:
I didn't actually buy anything from here but I had never check it out so I decided to take a look. 

Western Cycle is a huge bike store and probably the best place to get a bike. When I was younger and was into bike riding I would always think of Canadian Tire when I needed a new bike but I wish I knew about this place back then. It's a bikers haven. They have all kinds of bikes for everyone and they range from about $100 to $5000. It seems like the perfect place for people who are passionate about biking.

The staff seemed to know everything about the bikes and other bikes in general. Aside from bicycles they also have biking accessories like clothes, helmets, etc. They also have a service department to take care of your bike maintenance for you or even fix it up.

I don't bike anymore so I would probably never go here but I highly recommend it to all people that love riding bikes.
history[4]:
Staples is great. They have a huge selection of office supplies for great prices. 

I don't know why I think office supplies are so cool but I love going to these stores and checking out cool pens or notepads and buying them for absolutely no reason. Staples also carries good office furniture that is frequently placed on sale so you can get great bargains when setting up your home office. 

It's also a really great place for back to school shopping supplies.

history[0]: I love that this place is close to work and they have a drive through.  The service has been nice, to me.  Sometimes it can be busy, but what do you expect on McCarran?
history[1]: First off.  Our server was great!  We didn't eat the Chinese food (I will come back and update my review for that because the menu looked good) we came for sushi.  The fish was fresh, miso a tad salty.  What got me was the lack of a selection for nigiri (i.e. no scallops?)  I was a little surprised because everyones been talking about this place.  I'm a bit of a sushi snob and if you are one too, you may be happier at places I frequent.  I don't go for the cooked rolls and most their rolls have something to likes of Crystal shrimp or crab in it.  I opted to special order my rolls (they had no problem doing). The place is nice enough.  I'm interested in eating the Chinese food next time for sure.  3 stars because of the Nigiri selection :)
history[2]: Thank you thank you! Had a small mixup with my lunch today.  Called and talked with Wes a manager.  He fixed everything!  I shop, eat and drink at whole foods almost on a daily basis as I live close by.  I always have a great experience.  The food is really good, a lot of selection for on the go as well and of course I love the Tap Room.  I just wanted to say thank you Wes keep doing what you're doing this is a great store!.
history[3]: This was soooo much fun.  My first time at this event and it won't be my last.  Amazing beer festival and thanks to yelp and Michael T, I was able to attend the event on the house! I thought everyone was in good sprits and enjoying the evening.  One thing I thought was a great idea during the breaks of the band and also a tad disrespectful (because they had them on while the bands were on stage) was the heads phones with the music on the dance floor.  Interesting concept.  I tasted some new brews I haven't had before-had a great time!
history[4]: Not impressed. I came for the Jjamppong. It's one of my favorites.Where's the seafood? 1 big shrimp and the rest were tiny little shrimp. 1 half tiny crab that you really couldn't eat and a few mussels. Wayy overpriced for the portion I was given. 15$ is too much. I'm comparing to it a place I go to for jiamppong in sparks ($12 and a very large bowl with real seafood is more like it.) Music was a tad to loud. There was no one there. We should have been able to chose where we wanted to sit, but we were sat right under the tv where the music came from. Our waitress was nice- she came out to greet us to make sure we were coming for noodles because the sign hadn't been changed yet.  Some staff seemed to pay more attention to their friends that came in then us. I did notice they were on yelp. I hoped they would see we were checked in and pay a little more attention we had to ask for a to- go box. This business is in competition with another noodle joint a few doors down. Should be interesting to see how it goes.

history[0]: Step out of your comfort zone and try this restaurant,  you won't be disappointed.  I drive by this location often while on the road for work and decided to make a go for it today.  I was dining alone so it can be a little uncomfortable walking into the unknown.  The staff was very friendly,  answered all of my questions and had my food ready quickly. I was greeted with a nice warm basket of pita and pickles (pickled veggies).  I ordered Foule from the appetizer menu which consists of whole chick peas, spices, lemon juice and some of the best olive oil I have ever had the pleasure of tasting.  It was so rich in flavor and color. I enjoyed it with the warm pita.  Next was my chicken kabob "sandwich" which was nice hearty chunks of grilled chicken, green and red peppers rolled into another large pita. It's alot of food so more than likely you will be asking for a to go box. The atmosphere was nice and clean and I can not wait to try more menu items.
history[1]: I wasn't overwhelmingly impressed with this restaurant.  There was plenty of parking which is nice but I believe "the largest waterfront restaurant" is potentially working against this establishment.  There were so many different levels and dining rooms that when we sat ourselves it took entirely too long to be noticed.  We were seated near an outside bar and the bartender was MIA for quite a while.  Our server/bartender was nice but not over the top. We started off with of course gator bites.  They were nice and tender and pretty flavorful. My husband and I shared a burger but they brought us the wrong one.  I mentioned it to the server but told him it was ok we would just stick with the one he brought us.  It was cooked medium piled high with baked Mac n cheese.  Kinda of odd but it worked.  He did remove the burger from our check which I did appreciate.  Overall it was a decent late lunch but nothing really stood out to me.  The boat docks could use some work, they need more slips. It does look like they are possibly repairing then currently which will be nice.
history[2]: Delicious! This is my 2nd visit, I'm sure I'll be back for more.  I found myself craving the cilantro rice.  This rotisserie style restaurant doesn't overwhelm you with too many menu choices, they do chicken and they do it well.  My first visit I had the chicken sandwich and I will say it was a little more dry than the white meat chicken breast and wing I tried today.  It was so juicy and flavorful.  I was offered a "yellow" sauce which was nice and garlicky, a nice addition to the chicken as well as rice.  I asked for a "green" sauce as well just for comparison.  The green is considered their spicy sauce.  I love spicy but I think the yellow was my fave.  Prices are a little steep but worth it for quality food.
history[3]:
I absolutely love this hometown local bar. The Local overs LOCAL brews as well as vodkas how Cool! 

The atmosphere is clean, with a pool table, dartboards, boardgames and the friendliest staff ever. Sherry is always so pleasant remembers your name and just makes it a special experience. They offer trivia/questionarre on Tuesdays too. 

This is a locals hangout with a dog friendly front patio area. Love the Local and I can't wait to return.
history[4]: Thus far, this is my favorite brewery that I have visited here in Tampa.  It's a small bar area with an eclectic vibe.  I love the local paintings and pictures on the wall. I loved the wonderfully flavored beers they offer.  Such interesting mix ups such as peanut butter YUM! I have a feeling I will enjoying these beers often, can't wait to return!

history[0]: I meaaaaan I feel like from the good reviews that maybe I'm an anomaly for having a bad experience. Perhaps I need to see this place in the light of day, it could be a different vibe entirely on a different night too. But I honestly didn't have a very good time on a Friday night with my friends. I thought the bartenders were uppity and snobbish and honestly I stared at a bartender for 20 minutes without any kind of acknowledgement that I existed. Finally my boyfriend caught his attention and he was kind of rude when he asked a question. He basically shoved the menu at him like "here, look, I don't have time for you" even though it really wasn't that busy of a night. I'm not sure if I'd go back...
history[1]:
Villagio tries to do what CineBistro has mastered. A unique, upscale movie and dining experience. However it does fall short on a few things. 

What I love: Villagio's cheaper ticket and menu prices. CineBistro is a bit ridiculous on this front. Second, they win hands down on their AUTOMATICALLY RECLINING seats. Super comfortable. 

What they miss the mark on: Luxury. It feels cheap. From the crinkly paper menu, to the ugly florescent bulbs that pop on when the waiters runs in, to the smell of wing sauce that mysteriously never went away the whole two hours, to the way that NONE of the previous viewers messes were cleaned up prior to letting guests enter the theater. And after I sat down no one came to clean it up for a good 15 minutes. It just didn't feel fancy, didn't feel luxury. It just left an OK impression in my mind.

And for the waitress saying everyone raves about the pizza being really good, it was NOT. Sauce tasted old and not homemade and the crust was tasteless and unimpressive. So, overall, not a big hit with me.
history[2]: I mean, it is exactly what you think it will be. It's the "club" of central ave. The blue light pulls in people from the street (much like a bug zapper, if you ask me) and the crowd that particularly haunts this place is dressy but I wouldn't say that goes hand in hand with classy. It's honestly quite a hard place to hang out as the music is too loud to talk but it's not really a good place to dance as the dance floor is much like a landing strip and is constantly busy with foot traffic going back and forth from the front of the club toward the back. The only way to do this place is probably with a private booth, but if you're looking for a place to hang with your friends in downtown St. Pete I mean I would say keep looking, unless you're into this kind of vibe.
history[3]: I'm hooked on this pizza! It's so different and it's pizza you can feel good about. I got the wheat crust and it was great taste and texture. They have a menu to cater to all kinds of diets too, lots of gluten free options. My only complaint was that there wasn't enough pizza for a personal serving, I wanted more! Probably because it just tasted so good. I also got the pear gorgonzola walnut salad for a side and I was glad I did. I left feeling satisfied but not like I just pigged out on pizza, it's almost guilt-free. This place is a winner in my book! Can't wait to go back.
history[4]: I really enjoyed my experience at Paddywagon. Really nice, clean bar and they had fun trivia you could play with your phone and purse hooks and outlets under the bar! (if you're like me an have a terrible battery that doesn't last through the night then this is amazing.) They also allow dogs in the bar? And they have a pool table and darts, it's a small space but it was really chill and had a diverse/good crowd for a saturday night.

history[0]: Delicious, cheap Chinese food. Usually drop in about once a week. A friend recommended a chicken egg Foo young, and I'm completely hooked. It's the only thing I order and I have no plans to change. Food and a drink for $5 is hard to beat.
history[1]: I've been to my share of chaat houses, but after seeing this place with it's 5 stars and 200+ reviews, I knew I had to check it out. This place did not disappoint. The Pakistani owner is hilarious and attentative. Seating is limited but we had 6 people and felt fine. The chaat is flavorful, unique, and satisfying. We've been raving about it since. Really looking forward to the summer to experience more of the lassi flavors. Mango is a solid choice, but the cardimum was a show stealer. I would recommend this place to locals and travelers.
history[2]:
Found a local Chinese place that's close, cheap, and tastes great.

First order I had the beef and broccoli with a hot and sour soup. The portions are large, enough to have three full meals from a single entree. The beef was average, I'd say, with really fresh, crisp broccoli with a not overbearing sauce. The hot and sour soup was tasty, maybe a little more liquid than some I've had before, and has the addition of mushrooms, which was nice, but unexpected.

The second time, I went with the chicken and broccoli with brown rice and this dish stood out. The chicken is all white meat with no fat or tendons. The brown rice is a nice addition where you have a hint of the husks, but tastes great. Once again, huge portions will allow me to enjoy the dish for a few more days.

The staff is friendly. I'm able to order my food and pick it up 15 minutes later. So far, had not had an issue with my order, which is nice change compared to some other local eat-outs. Prices are very reason with an entree and rice for under $10.
history[3]: This is some of the best fried chicken in the city. Really tender pieces. I'm normally not a skin person, but the crispy flavor is just right. Definitely worth the wait and the trek over.
history[4]:
Saw a fair amount of mixed reviews. Did my homework and found that they had a good deal on Falken tires with installation, road hazard, and lifetime balance. Talked to Mike, who was helpful through the process. Initially they said they only had 3 tires in stock and had to pick up the fourth, and it would take a few hours. I was short on time and told them I'd come back in the AM. The manager, Milken, dropped by, talked to a tech outside, came back and told me they could start now and have other tire sent over. Within 20 minutes, the car was on the rack. Talked to the tech who pulled my full sized spare and switched out the 15 year old tire for me. 

As the car was racked, the fourth tire arrived. They took the wheels to the back to mount and balance the new Falkens. They offered an alignment, which is typical for a tire shop, I declined since my previous tires have no signs of uneven wear and I have no steering issues. 

I came around 4:30p and tires were mounted by 6:15p. They brought out the torque wrench and tightened down all the lugs, which was nice to see; some places don't. 

Overall, had a positive experience. Friendly staff, quick service, a good deal, and no issues. Can't speak for maintenance, but would be back for any tire issue in the future.

history[0]: Bought a headlight there the other day and they helped me replace it, too. They made helpful recommendations that saved me money. I would definitely stop there again if necessary! And there car freshners smell amazing!
history[1]: The food is pretty good, and I like that they have the soup, rice, and bread with meal; but it would be nice to have more sauces to choose from. Not the best I've been to, but not disappointing either!
history[2]: It's a pretty small mall with a few good stores, but also a lot of cheap clothing stores. The theater is decent, as well as the food court with a variety of options. The biggest downfall is the amount of kids who run around in there. It's obnoxious and generally you're better off at the Tucson Mall.
history[3]: Came here with some friends and had some drinks (soda for me lol!) They seemed to enjoy their drinks, and bragged about how good the food is here. They ordered a cheese quesadilla, which honestly was not my favorite, the cheese tasted like those string cheese things...but aside from that our waiter was friendly and the restaurant was nice. They had big tv's all around with music videos, games, news etc. It was pretty packed which normally drives me crazy, but at this place seemed fitting...after baby is born, would return for drinks!
history[4]: Came here twice before, and both times the food and service was great! I enjoy the Parmesan chicken, while others seem to enjoy the eggplant entrees. A word of advice, don't come right at dinner time, the place is popular and will have a long wait most nights at that time!

history[0]: The nicest people in the world work here.  I've purchased gardening goods and had a gazillion keys cut here and have never been disappointed or had to return a key because it didn't work.  I drive across the city to get keys made now instead of chancing a few trips to Home Depot. The amount of stuff packed into this place is impressive, and yet it doesn't feel cluttered.  Prices are also incredibly reasonable. Good stuff.
history[1]:
My brother took me to Philadelphia Archery and Gun Club for my first gun shooting experience.  He's a member here and has tried many of the shooting ranges around and in the city.  He brought along a 9mm and a rifle, an sks? or something.  I don't know I think it was of the Russian assault variety.  He gave me a lot of information in a short period of time and I mostly retained the stuff I needed to know immediately.  

We arrived and were greeted by some lackluster staff that had me show ID and fill out a form before directing us upstairs.  To be fair it was after 7:30 and they close up around 8:30 or 9.  The upstairs gentleman, Mike, was really nice.  He liked my name and said it reminded him of something from Sherlock Holmes or Hopalong Cassidy and then proceeded to show me his dvd case for Hopalong Cassidy.  It was weird but I liked it.   I don't know what would happen if I had gone here without a member or someone skilled with guns so I can't say much about the staff on that level but nice is nice.  It was $5 for me to shoot since I was with a member, apparently Wednesday is ladies night and ladies shoot free so keep that in mind if you were blessed with girly parts. 

The range is smallish, with about eight or so lanes.  Each is equipped with a target holder and controls for moving your target.  My brother brought zombie targets but the range has targets (for sale I think) if you need them.  According to my brother the ammunition is a little pricey but I don't know much about that.  I don't know that I'd go back and shoot again but it was a cool experience and I can scratch it off of my list.
history[2]:
First a little disclaimer, this review is for the drinks only, I've never eaten here, also the reason for the four stars.  I walk dogs in Red Hook's neighborhood so I often find myself nearing the end of the day in need of a pick me up or warm up within blocks of this gem.  

The decor is amazing and welcoming.  Really cute and comfortable furniture lines one wall while a bar with chairs lines the windowed wall.  The employees are NICE, something oddly lacking in a lot of Philly coffee shops.  They are also talented baristas and my drinks are consistent and damn tasty.  My favorite drink is their seasonal Ancho Chile Hot Chocolate- my god! It's pricey but you can taste why.  The drink is pure bliss, chocolatey and sweet, milky and smooth but with a spice and little bits of ancho chile to chew on between sips.  

I've also had plain coffee, of which you have a choice of roasts, and a few other fancy coffees.  Someday I'l try one of their paninis or vegan sweets and see if they are worthy of a fifth star.  

One time I stupidly spilled a large portion of my drink while trying to add cream and sugar  with the same hand I was using to hold a dog leash(did I mention you can take your doggie here).  The dog pulled and coffee went everywhere.  Fail.  The baristas were so nice, they didn't get mad at me, helped me clean up and refilled my coffee.  Yet another reason why I go here more than any of my local coffee places and tip every time.
history[3]:
This place made me swear off restaurant week. Usually my husband and I avoid busy restaurants on Fridays and Saturdays and opt for a Thursday date night, we have also sworn off Valentine's Day and any other amateur hour/prix fixe/everyone is out type of night. We were still clinging to restaurant week, but have been let down on so many occasions.  Bummer. 

We started off with drinks, and there is something off about the drinks here. I can't place it, but they just taste awful, and we had three different drinks. The worst was the blood orange martini. It tasted so flat and bland and almost watered down. The Pepino, the jalapeño/cucumber/basil beverage should have been packed with flavor, but fell flat with just heat and little actual taste. It was the best of the three. I then tried the cherry grand marnier drink, can't remember the name as recommended by the manager--again it fell flat and though it had been described to me as "sweet" it was not at all. I could taste the alcohol hardcore, but little else. I suspect they are using low quality tequilas and charging top shelf prices, but that's just my theory. 

We placed our order-- guac that we customized, cheese empanadas, mexican flatbread, two vegetable sopas and the churros and chocolate tres leches cake for dessert. Our guac arrived and again, I only tasted the heat of the habanero that we had requested. We also asked for spanish onions, roasted garlic and tomatoes. I have made better guacamole whilst drunk. BLAND city. I even squeezed a lime in it form my drink to brighten the flavor, nothing. It was also way too smooth, like they had a vat of pureed avocado in the back and were just adding things in quickly. The chips also needed more salt. 

Next, we received our entrees instead of our apps. When the guy (not our waiter) put them down in front of us we were all "nope, didn't get apps yet" and he was all ok, bye and left the entrees there. Uh what? The manager did come over and apologize and offer us a free drink for the mistake, encourage us to eat meal in reverse. We did, and it kind of ruined the whole night since we filled up like crazy on the heavy entree. We then waited quite some time for our apps. The empanadas were the only amazing part of the meal. The flatbread had an odd mouthfeel to it, was greasy and again lacked flavor. We waited awhile again for our desserts and our waiter was mostly MIA for the entire meal.  Finally dessert. The churros were not fully cooked. The oil was obviously too hot because they outsides were crispy and brown, but they were pure uncooked batter inside. Gross. This was confirmed by the table next to us, as theirs were also partially raw. The cake was yummy. Subpar meal, bad drinks, kind of crappy service. I did appreciate the effort from the manager, but he has some work to do. Wish I'd tried this place before it changed hands.
history[4]:
It's huge, it's awesome, it's open! South Philadelphia has been my usual wine and spirits stomping ground, but now my liver is really in for it with this gem open in the neighborhood.  The wine selection is amazing, as is pretty much the entire selection of every liquor.  The first day they were open I bought a bottle of St. Germain, a handle of Bacardi select, and a bottle of wine, for under $65, so the prices are pretty lovely as well.  The staff was super helpful and knowledgeable of the product and store layout. 

This is more than a step up, this is a whole escalator up from the liquor store we used to have to deal with in Northern Liberties.  Mad Dog varieties have been replaced with fine wines and spirits.  There's short-term parking on a Girard, and a parking lot behind the store.  It's like they actually care about their customers!

history[0]:
Came in for lunch and on their menu they have around 15 or so lunch specials from $5.99 to $6.99. Nice. Their staff is so cheerfully friendly. The chips are great, I enjoyed the salsas, one mild and one hot. And I enjoyed the freshly made guac for $2.00 well spent. 4 stars.

My wife had the fish tacos, probably made of Tilapia. We're not huge fans of Tilapia as it is a tad fishy tasting. The tacos weren't bad, just not great, because of the Tilapia. It was small flanks of fish on a small street taco tortilla. She had a grilled and two breaded ones to try. 3 stars.

I had the tacos (?) can't remember the name, but the shells were crispy pan fried. It comes with three so I tried all options: a shredded beef, a hamburger, and a chicken. All were OK. Not great, OK. I actually didn't enjoy the shredded beef that much because since it was twice cooked, it was a bit fibrous and chewy. I just don't like shredded beef, I guess. Your mileage will vary. The hamburger one was OK and so was the chicken. It comes with lettuce, tomato, onion, cheese, and pico, but there is no way you can put it in the taco without breaking the shell into smithereens. So that was kind of a fail. I took a bite and forked some of the lettuce, tomato, onion, cheese, pico into my mouth. I also tried putting it on top of the taco shell sideways and eating it, but that wasn't successful, either. 3 stars.

history[1]:
This is one of many Sonoran dog stands that I'm sure are not listed in Yelp yet. This is on the corner of 1st Ave. and Prince in front of the carniceria. I've been passing by it now for several years and decided to try it today. Glad I did.

He does his Chipilon style, which means he toasts the bun. That's the way I like it. And of course, he uses Alejandro's buns. This is a very good Sonoran Dog. Well constructed, tasty, nothing overly done.

He also makes his own horchata and limonade. We had the horchata, Excellent. $1.50 for a medium and $3.00 for a "grande," which is gigantic.

Today was windy and he had the canopy set up over the tables. Made for more pleasant eating.

On Wednesdays he has a special of two dogs and a drink for $5.00. Good deal.

I highly recommend this food truck.
history[2]:
Well, as for me, there are no good Chinese restaurants on the east side of town. Once again, a high review at an Asian restaurant disappoints this Asian.

We ordered egg rolls, Kung Pao chicken and BBQ pork lo mein. The egg rolls were decently sized and were not bad. Not great, mind you, but not bad. The Kung Pao chicken was disappointing. The sauce was soy sauce based and the flavor was not wonderful. The chicken was a bit dry and fibrous. The lo mein had more bean sprouts than noodles. I really didn't taste any noodles nor did I have any noodle texture. Too much bean sprouts. The pork was decent, though.

The service was nice. She was polite, concerned and attentive. It is a nice restaurant.

Will not ever return.
history[3]:
OK, it's a cheap movie re-run theater. And it's good.

As a father of four children, we've been to our share of cheap theaters in the four states in which we lived. And this one is actually quite pleasant.

First of all, the price. $3.50 for regular tickets. Not going to break the bank. $3.00 for seniors, and at this theater, "seniors" is age 51 and older! $3.00 for kids. $1.50 on Tuesdays! So for a little more than the price of a Redbox disc (or on Tuesdays, the same price as a Redbox disc), you can see a movie on the big screen.

This theater was clean. Almost all of the cheap theaters my family has been to have been dirty. This one is clean.

Seats are comfortable. In our screen room, it wasn't stadium seating and I do not believe they have stadium seating at all. But the seats are comfortable and surprisingly look new-ish.

Sound system isn't the latest and greatest, but it sure is good enough. I don't feel ripped off in this category at all.

The movie quality is great. That must be because they are showing it digitally. Cheap theaters used to mean watching worn out 35mm reels and you could see the wear and tear in the movie with lines and visual static. Not here. Picture quality was as good as new.

Nice selection of movies, too. Usually at the cheap theaters, they are playing only two to four movies. Here, they have 14 movies playing. You get a nice menu choice.

Concessions are just as pricey as a regular theater. I will say that I did enjoy the Orville Redenbacher's brand popcorn and the Fanta Apple pop.

I'll be back.

5* = A top favorite. Mind-blowing good. I will definitely return and I will go out of my way to go here. Worth every dime of my money.
4* = Outstanding. Tucson-top-tier. Some elements not quite as good as it gets, but it still is worthy of my regular business.
3* = Good experience. I might do repeat business if it is convenient, the only option, the only one in its category, or on a whim, but would not be among the first thing to come to mind. Would definitely recommend you trying it to get your own take on it.
2* = Not so good. Would not recommend at all. Waste of money.
1* = How are they still in business? Stay away at all costs!
history[4]:
Came here because a dear friend gave me a gift card.

I'm sorry to say that Allegro il Gelato has completely spoiled my tastebuds. Completely. Obviously Frost has struck a chord here in Tucson, with all of its fans giving high reviews here and its many franchised stores. Me thinks that many, if most, have not tried Allegro's.

OK, I start sampling flavors. I think I'm going to try as many in-common flavors as Allegro's for a fun comparison. Start with one of my perennial favorites--salted caramel. Yuck. Tastes artificial. OK, cinnamon next. Nope. The same. This is not good. Two of what should be home run flavors foul out. Maybe a fruit flavor will be better. Strawberry. Ugh. Tastes like grocery strawberries. (Coming from CA, that's NOT a compliment. How *does* Ivan at Allegro Gelato get his to taste so much like fresh CA freshberries?!!!) Only three flavors and already my mouth is "frosted."

My wife tried Peanut Butter Crunch, Pumpkin, and Cinnamon. She said they all kind of tasted the same. She loves anything peanut butter. She passes.

She ends up with Cookies and Cream. It's OK. In desperation, I try chocolate. Acceptable.

After experiencing Allegro Gelato, it was disconcerting to watch them just slap the gelato in the cup. Almost unappetizing. I'm thinking, "You're not slinging mortar to lay bricks! Isn't your product better than that?"

I liked the flakes of chocolate in my flavor. It gave it a little nice texture to it. But let's compare other qualities. The texture of Frost in this chocolate was not bad. The texture in the other flavors were more "airy" than at Allegro. One thing that struck me was that with Allegro's gelato, there is a wonderful after taste that lingers on the taste buds like a romantic memory on my palate. 'Twas not so with Frost. The flavor was only present when it was in my mouth.

Like Allegro, Frost has a round cookie (see photos) they put in the cup of gelato. It is a cheaper cookie than the hazelnut cream filled pirouettes you get at Allegro. I mean it's a good cookie, but I'm comparing here, and it is not of the quality at Allegro.

The spoons. Ah, here is where they outshine Allegro. They are multi-colored mini spade-like paddle things. And bigger than what Allegro uses. They are kind of cool.

Prices are comparable with Allegro. Service is really good. My server always called me "sir." Many employees on a Friday night made for fast service.

So bottom line is Frost is OK. At least the chocolate. The rest tastes like medicine to me. Will you Frost fans, who obviously have fallen in love with gelato, go give Ivan's gelato a try? He's not only Italian (like the owners of Frost, I understand) but he got his degree in Italy at the gelato university in Bologna.

Two or three stars?....Oh...I don't know...."Meh, I've experienced better"...Yeah, I guess so.

I thought over this rating for quite some time, and also ran it by my boyfriend who accompanied me here this morning.  I was hesitant to rate this at 2 stars especially due to the other reviews that came before me (4 stars, 5 stars, etc)- which, by the way, was WHY we came here in the first place rather than going somewhere else.  Im going to start right off the bat with what I and my boyfriend ordered.  I ordered a cappuccino (I have been ordering them EVERYWHERE lately so I base my experience on everywhere else in fishtown/no libs that offers a cappuccino) and my boyfriend ordered his usual plain iced coffee (he orders it even in the dead of winter so he's tried an iced coffee all over as well).  Ill get to the point, my cappuccino was 'eh'- it tasted burnt and unpure.  I'm no coffee connoisseur but I do know a good cup when I taste one and this wasn't it.  My boyfriend said his iced coffee tasted watered down and unremarkable.  He ordered an everything bagel with butter and I just opted for a yogurt because thats what I was in the mood for.  The bagel was also 'eh' and the butter literally tasted like nothing (not sure if that's what theyre going for or not but it was unremarkable as well).  All in all, that's exactly our experience this morning- unremarkable.  I hate leaving reviews like this because I want to give places the benefit of the doubt and maybe we just didn't order a good signature coffee that theyre known for or maybe we should have tried a pastry but I had to go by the rating system and since I went by the rating system, the experience was "Meh. I've experienced better."  The prices are better than other coffee shops in the area but that doesnt mean the quality should lack. I would probably give this place 5 stars if I had much experience with other places but this was my first time ever getting a colonic.  Also, my boyfriends experience was just a bit different than mine (we went together, at the same time, in two different rooms).

My room was awesome...it had the bed and the 'cleaning station' (with wipes and whatnot for you to use afterwards) and next to the bed was a mirror that was angled upwards so you can see what was coming out of you!  Of course it sounds weird but I loved it! I enjoyed watching my body get rid of all that stuff- it was reassuring that I was actually DOING something for my body.  I was also given this awesome heat pack for my stomach for when I had minor cramping (best heat pack ever...I want to know where they get those things! It keeps the heat for EVER!)  I was checked in on by two different ladies (Cheryl and her assistant Ruth- I believe those names are correct but I could be wrong, im horrible with names).  Both were friendly and accommodating.  

Downsides: my boyfriend tells me afterwards that he did NOT have the viewing ability.  There was no mirror and no tube where he can see what was being flushed out.  Is this right?  I told him maybe he missed it and didn't notice it but I feel that he would have noticed something like that.  Maybe only one room was equipped with this.  That was a huge bummer because I feel like that was a huge part of the experience! He also didn't get the heat pack for some reason and I think he could have used it because he had slight cramping as well.  

I wish that Cheryl could have maybe spoken to us (or rather just me because I had the viewing tube) about what I was seeing.   I thought that maybe after she would but that never happened.  Next time maybe I will ask but I know that each color and consistency is very important and means different things.  I was hoping that I could leave that place with the recommendation of eating more of one thing or less of another or something based off of my excrement (eww gross I know, but its true!)

Price is ok, wish it was less so that we could do it more often.  Definitely want to go back for a second time around to really get more stuff out- we just need to wait for the paycheck, its about $55 per person plus tip for a basic hydrotherapy colonic session (smallest package)
